exercises.title: Hybrid KEM Implementation
exercises.description: Implement a hybrid KEM combining X25519 and ML-KEM.
tasks: Use proper key derivation for combining secrets
exercises.starterCode:
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Tuple
import hashlib
import os

@dataclass
class HybridKeyPair:
    classical_pk: bytes
    classical_sk: bytes
    pqc_pk: bytes
    pqc_sk: bytes

class MockX25519:
    """Mock X25519 for demonstration."""
    PK_SIZE = 32
    SK_SIZE = 32
    CT_SIZE = 32
    SS_SIZE = 32
    
    def keygen(self) -> Tuple[bytes, bytes]:
        sk = os.urandom(32)
        pk = hashlib.sha256(b'x25519_pk' + sk).digest()
        return pk, sk
    
    def encapsulate(self, pk: bytes) -> Tuple[bytes, bytes]:
        ephemeral = os.urandom(32)
        ct = hashlib.sha256(b'x25519_ct' + ephemeral).digest()
        ss = hashlib.sha256(b'x25519_ss' + pk + ephemeral).digest()
        return ct, ss
    
    def decapsulate(self, sk: bytes, ct: bytes) -> bytes:
        # In real X25519, this would be proper ECDH
        return hashlib.sha256(b'x25519_ss_dec' + sk + ct).digest()

class MockMLKEM:
    """Mock ML-KEM for demonstration."""
    PK_SIZE = 1184
    SK_SIZE = 2400
    CT_SIZE = 1088
    SS_SIZE = 32
    
    def keygen(self) -> Tuple[bytes, bytes]:
        sk = os.urandom(self.SK_SIZE)
        pk = os.urandom(self.PK_SIZE)
        return pk, sk
    
    def encapsulate(self, pk: bytes) -> Tuple[bytes, bytes]:
        ct = os.urandom(self.CT_SIZE)
        ss = hashlib.sha256(b'mlkem_ss' + pk).digest()
        return ct, ss
    
    def decapsulate(self, sk: bytes, ct: bytes) -> bytes:
        return hashlib.sha256(b'mlkem_ss_dec' + sk[:32]).digest()

class HybridKEM:
    """X25519 + ML-KEM Hybrid KEM."""
    
    def __init__(self):
        self.x25519 = MockX25519()
        self.mlkem = MockMLKEM()
    
    def keygen(self) -> HybridKeyPair:
        """Generate hybrid key pair."""
        c_pk, c_sk = self.x25519.keygen()
        p_pk, p_sk = self.mlkem.keygen()
        return HybridKeyPair(c_pk, c_sk, p_pk, p_sk)
    
    def encapsulate(self, keypair: HybridKeyPair) -> Tuple[bytes, bytes]:
        """
        Encapsulate to produce ciphertext and shared secret.
        TODO: Implement this
        """
        pass
    
    def decapsulate(self, keypair: HybridKeyPair, ct: bytes) -> bytes:
        """
        Decapsulate to recover shared secret.
        TODO: Implement this
        """
        pass
    
    def _combine_secrets(self, ss1: bytes, ss2: bytes) -> bytes:
        """Combine shared secrets using HKDF-like construction."""
        # Simple combination - use HKDF in production
        return hashlib.sha256(
            b'hybrid_kem_v1' + ss1 + ss2
        ).digest()

# Test
hybrid = HybridKEM()
kp = hybrid.keygen()
print(f"Generated hybrid keypair")
print(f"  Classical PK: {len(kp.classical_pk)} bytes")
print(f"  PQC PK: {len(kp.pqc_pk)} bytes")
exercises.expectedOutput: Working hybrid KEM with successful round-trip
hints: Ciphertext is concatenation of both ciphertexts
Use domain separation in secret combination
Test that decapsulation returns same secret as encapsulation

exercises.title: Migration Planning Tool
exercises.description: Build a tool to assess and plan PQC migration.
tasks: Calculate size impact of PQC migration
exercises.expectedOutput: Working migration planning tool with reports
hints: Consider different asset types (TLS, code signing, PKI)

exercises.title: Interoperability Matrix
exercises.description: Create an interoperability compatibility matrix for PQC algorithms.
tasks: List major PQC implementations and libraries

exercises.title: Size Impact Calculator
exercises.description: Calculate the size impact of migrating protocols to PQC.
exercises.expectedOutput: Size impact calculator with visual report
hints: TLS handshake includes certificates and key exchange
Consider both key and signature sizes
Compare hybrid vs pure PQC sizes
